question_title: Will the ECDC weekly SARS-CoV-2 test positivity series show four consecutive week-over-week increases for the EU/EEA, before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether EU/EEA SARS-CoV-2 test positivity will show 4 consecutive week-over-week increases during weeks W06-W17 of 2026. Direct analysis of the ECDC CSV data shows current positivity at 2.0% (W05) and steadily declining. In the 4 historical years with data for this window (2022-2025), only 2023 achieved 4+ consecutive increases, and that year entered W06 on a rising trend. In years matching 2026's conditions (low positivity + declining trend entering W06), 0 out of 4 such sliding windows produced 4+ consecutive increases. At very low positivity levels, the data exhibits mean-reverting noise that frequently breaks streaks. Even during 2025's spring wave onset, the maximum was only 2 consecutive increases. Monte Carlo simulations give 8-18% depending on autocorrelation assumptions. I estimate ~15%, accounting for the possibility of an atypically early spring wave from a new variant, but weighting the strong historical evidence against.

factors.description: Historical base rate: only 1/4 years (2023) had 4+ consecutive increases in W06-W17 window
2026 entering W06 on declining trend with very low positivity (2.0%), matching 2024/2025 pattern where 0/2 had 4+ increases
At low positivity (<5%), data shows mean-reverting sawtooth pattern - 2025 spring wave onset maxed at 2 consecutive increases
Spring waves in 2024/2025 started after W17, so 4-consecutive-increase runs occurred outside the question window
Monte Carlo IID model with empirical W06-W17 increase rate (33%) gives ~8% probability
Possibility of new immune-evasive variant causing atypically early spring wave (like 2023)
12 weeks provides reasonable number of opportunities for a streak to form

Let me check for any current COVID variant news that could change the outlook.Now let me write my meta-reflection and produce the final forecast.**Forecast: 15% probability (Yes)**

Key findings from analyzing the actual ECDC CSV data:

- **Current state**: EU/EEA SARS-CoV-2 positivity is at 2.0% (W05 2026), steadily declining from 3.8% at W01.
- **Historical base rate**: Only 1 of 4 years (2023) had 4+ consecutive increases during W06-W17. That year entered W06 on a *rising* trend — unlike 2026.
- **Closest analogues**: In years matching 2026's conditions (low positivity + declining trend entering W06) — 2024 and 2025 — both maxed at only 2 consecutive increases in the W06-W17 window.
- **Low-level noise**: At <5% positivity, the EU/EEA aggregate shows a mean-reverting sawtooth pattern that breaks streaks. Even the 2025 spring wave onset only managed 2 consecutive increases.
- **Timing**: Spring waves in both 2024 and 2025 started *after* W17, placing 4-consecutive-increase runs outside the question window.
- **Upside risk**: A new variant could trigger an atypically early spring wave (as in 2023), which is the main path to Yes.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves Yes if, after the time the question opens and before May 1, 2026, the EU/EEA weekly SARS-CoV-2 test positivity values in the ECDC/ERVISS weekly dataset contain at least one run of four consecutive reporting weeks where each week’s positivity is strictly greater than the immediately prior week’s positivity. The primary source is the ECDC/ERVISS weekly data CSV “SARITestsDetectionsPositivity.csv” (EU-ECDC GitHub), which must be used to evaluate week labels and positivity values: [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/EU-ECDC/Respiratory\_viruses\_weekly\_data/refs/heads/main/data/SARITestsDetectionsPositivity.csv](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/EU-ECDC/Respiratory_viruses_weekly_data/refs/heads/main/data/SARITestsDetectionsPositivity.csv).
fine_print:
For purposes of this question, the first week being looked at is 2026-W06 and the final week is 2026-W17.

“Consecutive weeks” means consecutive reporting weeks in the dataset’s week field with no gaps. If multiple EU/EEA aggregates exist in the file, use the aggregate explicitly labeled for EU/EEA (or “EU/EEA total”) for SARS-CoV-2 positivity; if no EU/EEA aggregate exists, this question resolves No. If the primary file format changes, use the closest successor file in the same repository that clearly contains the same EU/EEA weekly SARS-CoV-2 positivity series; if no such successor is available by resolution time, resolve using the backup candidate source below. Candidate Sources (backup, used only if needed): ECDC ERVISS summary/links page: [https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/publications-data/european-respiratory-virus-surveillance-summary-erviss](https://www.ecdc.europa.eu/en/publications-data/european-respiratory-virus-surveillance-summary-erviss).
background_info:
This forecasts whether European SARS-CoV-2 test positivity exhibits a sustained multi-week rise, which can signal accelerating transmission and pressure on health systems.
